description: The intake gate for the Email Engine. The questions are asked ALL AT ONE TIME, in a single message block, BEFORE any email is written. Once answered, the locked brief (working/copy/brief.json) is written against this contract and P1 SELECT unlocks. This file is the authoritative spec that prove-email.py loads for the intake gate. The runtime answers are recorded against runtime_intake_contract; a self-attested completeness flag is NEVER trusted — the brief must come from this turn-gated ledger.

delivery.rule: Emit all six required questions (plus the two optional branch questions) as ONE message-block turn. Splitting them across turns fails AF-EMAIL-INTAKE-SPLIT.
delivery.front_door: any department/role that needs an email or sequence (Skill 38 request, Skill 49 post-Downsell trigger, Command Center task ingest)

questions.prompt: What is the objective of this email / sequence? (promotional, abandoned-cart, upsell, or downsell)
Who is the buyer? (spontaneous, methodical, humanistic, or competitive) — for a full 12-email sequence say 'all' and we map each buyer band to its frameworks.
questions.note: Allowed single values: spontaneous | methodical | humanistic | competitive | all. Drives the buyer-type -> email# -> framework map for 12-email sequences.

questions.prompt: What is the exact offer / product / event being promoted? (the exact name(s) become the offer-token ledger)
What is the brand voice / tone to continue? (paste a sample or describe it — punchy, warm, authoritative, etc.)
Where does this live? A single email, or a full sequence? (single | landing-page-10-promo | high-ticket-appointment | convert-and-flow-buyer-12). For a single email, which slot/framework?
questions.note: Resolves to sequence_type: single | landing_page_10 | high_ticket_12 | buyer_type_12. Declares the expected preview count and subject char-band mode the prover reads.

questions.prompt: What is the founder's ACTUAL name for the closing signature? (no placeholder — this exact name must appear in the close)
Is this a high-ticket appointment-booking sequence? (yes / no) — high-ticket uses two preview lines and the 80-87-char emoji subject band.
Optional: adopt a persona STYLE for high-ticket / Upsell-2 emails? (one of the 12 styles, tone only — the person is NEVER named or quoted). Leave blank to skip.

runtime_intake_contract.note: Shape of working/copy/brief.json that prove-email.py validates in intake mode. Any violation is a nonzero exit, fail-closed.

required_fields.question_block_msg_id: single non-empty id proving the questions were emitted in ONE block (else AF-EMAIL-INTAKE-SPLIT)
required_fields.answers: object carrying non-empty objective, buyer_type, offer, brand_voice, sequence_position, founder_name (any missing/empty -> AF-EMAIL-BRIEF-INCOMPLETE); objective must be a valid objective value

optional_fields.persona_style: one of the 12 canonical style ids
optional_fields.offer_token_ledger: array of the exact offer name(s) from the offer answer
optional_fields.client_overrode_word_band: boolean — set true ONLY when the client states an exact per-email length that overrides the 150-300 default; surfaced on the process certificate

autofail_codes.AF-EMAIL-TYPE-MISMATCH: skill != email-engine, or an unknown sequence_type
autofail_codes.AF-EMAIL-INTAKE-SPLIT: the questions were not delivered as ONE block (asked_all_at_once not true / one_question_per_turn true / multiple block ids)
autofail_codes.AF-EMAIL-BRIEF-INCOMPLETE: any of the six required brief fields missing or empty, or an invalid objective value
